milestones.title: Risk Retirement & Subsystem Proof
milestones.vision: Prove every critical subsystem works independently — Alvara factory discovery and BSKT creation, deBridge cross-chain bridging, Bags SDK fee share capture, Jupiter swaps, holder resolution — then wire the outbound path end-to-end.

slices.title: Alvara Factory Discovery & BSKT Proof
slices.demo: A BSKT exists on Alvara's Base deployment created programmatically via the discovered factory contract, verifiable on Basescan
slices.goal: Discover Alvara's factory contract on Base from public on-chain data and prove programmatic BSKT creation works via standard contract calls
slices.success_criteria:
Factory contract address discovered on Base from public on-chain transaction data
Factory ABI identified from published ERC-7621 interface and on-chain function signatures
EIP-1967 proxy status determined for factory contract
BSKT created programmatically on Base OR MEV protection signing requirements documented with integration path
If BSKT created: ERC-7621 compliance verified (supportsInterface, getConstituents, totalSupply, owner)
All discovered addresses and ABIs persisted to machine-readable JSON config
MEV integration findings documented regardless of creation outcome
slices.proof_level: contract — real on-chain transactions on Base mainnet proving factory integration works (or documenting exactly what the MEV signing flow requires to integrate properly)
slices.integration_closure:
Upstream surfaces consumed: Basescan API, Base RPC, Alvara factory contract (public on-chain interface)
New wiring introduced: discovered-contracts.json config consumed by all downstream Alvara integration modules
What remains: S02-S04 prove other subsystems independently; S05 wires factory module into fund backend
slices.observability_impact:
npm install completes without errors
npx tsx scripts/discover-factory.ts exits 0 and prints JSON containing a factoryAddress field with a valid 0x-prefixed 40-hex-char address
cat src/config/discovered-contracts.json contains factoryAddress, abi (non-empty array), isProxy (boolean), and chainId (8453 for Base)
npx tsx scripts/create-bskt.ts exits 0 with bsktAddress and txHash, OR exits 1 with src/config/mev-findings.json containing mevRequired: true and an integration recommendation
If BSKT created: npx tsx scripts/verify-bskt.ts verifies ERC-7621 compliance
src/config/mev-findings.json exists with structured integration findings

slices.title: deBridge Solana→Base Bridge Proof
slices.demo: USDC bridged from Solana to Base via deBridge DLN API with verifiable tx hashes on both chains
slices.goal: Prove deBridge DLN cross-chain bridging works for Solana→Base USDC transfers
slices.success_criteria: USDC arrives on Base wallet from Solana via deBridge
slices.integration_closure: Bridge module reusable by outbound pipeline

slices.title: Bags SDK Fee Share & Reflection Claiming
slices.demo: Fee share for a test token redirected to a treasury wallet and accumulated fees claimed via Bags SDK
slices.goal: Prove Bags SDK fee share redirection and reflection claiming works programmatically
slices.success_criteria: Fee share redirected and reflections claimed
slices.integration_closure: Bags SDK module reusable by pipeline
slices.observability_impact: Fee share config and claim tx hashes

slices.title: Jupiter Swap & Holder Resolution
slices.demo: SOL swapped to USDC via Jupiter and top 100 holders resolved for an arbitrary SPL token mint
slices.goal: Prove Jupiter swaps and Solana holder resolution work
slices.success_criteria: Swap completes and holder list resolved
slices.observability_impact: Swap tx hash and holder list

slices.title: Fund Backend & Data Model
slices.demo: PostgreSQL-backed service persisting fund instances with full state tracking, connected to all subsystem modules
slices.goal: Build the fund data model and persistence layer
slices.integration_closure: Data model consumed by pipeline orchestrator

slices.title: Outbound Subsystem Integration
slices.demo: End-to-end outbound flow: claim reflection → Jupiter swap to USDC → deBridge bridge to Base → USDC confirmed on Base
slices.integration_closure: Outbound pipeline ready for M002 automation
slices.observability_impact: Pipeline phase tracking and tx hashes

tasks.title: Scaffold project and discover Alvara factory contract on Base
tasks.description: Initialize the TypeScript project from scratch and write the factory discovery script that looks up Alvara's BSKT factory contract address from public on-chain data via Basescan. Identify the factory's public interface, detect proxy patterns, and persist findings to a JSON config consumed by T02 and all downstream integration work.
tasks.verify: npm install completes without errors AND npx tsx scripts/discover-factory.ts exits 0 with JSON containing factoryAddress (valid 0x address) AND cat src/config/discovered-contracts.json contains factoryAddress, abi (non-empty), isProxy (boolean), chainId (8453)

tasks.title: Create BSKT on Base via factory and verify ERC-7621 compliance
tasks.description: Using T01's discovered factory address and ABI, write the BSKT creation script and ERC-7621 verification module. Call the factory's public interface to create a basket. If the MEV protection layer requires backend-signed parameters, analyze recent successful creation transactions to understand the signing flow and document a clean integration path. Both success and MEV-documented outcomes are valid proof for this slice.
tasks.verify: npx tsx scripts/create-bskt.ts exits 0 with bsktAddress+txHash OR exits 1 with src/config/mev-findings.json containing mevRequired:true and integration recommendation AND if BSKT created: npx tsx scripts/verify-bskt.ts verifies ERC-7621 compliance AND src/config/mev-findings.json exists with structured findings
tasks.observability_impact: Creation script logs { phase, action, txHash?, error?, revertReason? } to stdout. MEV analysis logs decoded calldata patterns. mev-findings.json persists integration findings for future agents. Revert reasons decoded and logged for diagnostic inspection.

decisions.choice: Base + Ethereum mainnet (fund creator chooses at setup)
decisions.rationale: Base has lower gas costs and fresh Alvara deployment (April 2026). Ethereum has deeper liquidity. User wants both.

decisions.choice: Reverse-engineer factory contracts from on-chain data, no team outreach
decisions.rationale: User explicitly chose "find it on chain" over contacting Alvara team. Avoids external dependency.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if on-chain discovery proves impossible due to MEV backend requirements

decisions.choice: deBridge DLN as primary, Wormhole deferred as potential fallback
decisions.rationale: deBridge has zero TVL risk (intent-based), 1-2s fulfillment, simple REST API, zero exploit history. Wormhole fallback deferred to reduce integration surface.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if deBridge proves unreliable on Solana↔Base route

decisions.choice: Immutable after fund creation — stored on-chain
decisions.rationale: Trust model: holders verify config once and know it's permanent. Owner can only change basket composition, not distribution split or triggers.

decisions.choice: No hard limit on active funds — each fund gets isolated state, wallets, and job queues
decisions.rationale: The app is generic for any Bags.fm token creator, not $BRAIN-specific. Isolated failure domains prevent one fund's issues from affecting others.
decisions.revisable: Yes — may add practical caps based on infrastructure costs

decisions.choice: Protocol fee on reflections — deducted on Solana side before bridge
decisions.rationale: User chose protocol fee over setup fees or Alvara management fee only. Deducting on Solana side is simplest — one SPL transfer before the pipeline runs.
decisions.revisable: Yes — percentage configurable at platform level

decisions.choice: Top 100 holders by token balance — no separate staking contract
decisions.rationale: User explicitly chose holders model. Simpler, aligns with Bags.fm existing top-100 dividend distribution model.
User specifically wants it embedded, not a standalone web app. Bags.fm app store embed format needs investigation in M004.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if Bags.fm doesn't support embedding

decisions.choice: Single cron job polling all active funds
decisions.rationale: Simpler than per-fund timers, fewer moving parts, easier to monitor and debug. One job iterates over all funds every 6 hours.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if fund count exceeds single-job processing window

decisions.choice: Repeating cycles — accumulate → invest → divest → distribute → repeat
decisions.rationale: Funds run continuously. Each divestment cycle distributes proceeds, then the fund starts accumulating again. No terminal state unless owner explicitly shuts down.

decisions.choice: Single registry contract per chain (Base and Ethereum)
decisions.rationale: One contract maps fund ID → immutable config struct. Cheaper gas than per-fund contracts. Single source of truth per chain.

decisions.choice: Route through Alvara's interface — reverse-engineer their backend API calls
decisions.rationale: Alvara's MEV protection uses a backend signing service for swap routes. Direct contract calls may bypass this protection and result in worse execution. Replicating their signed flow preserves MEV protection.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if M001/S01 reveals direct contract calls work fine for all operations

decisions.choice: Resume from failed step — each pipeline step is checkpointed
decisions.rationale: No wasted gas on completed steps. Each step records its completion state. On failure, retry from the exact failed step with exponential backoff.

decisions.choice: Snapshot at trigger time (before redemption + bridge)
decisions.rationale: Prevents gaming by buying tokens during the bridge delay window. Snapshot is locked when divestment fires.

decisions.choice: Distribute → immediately verify on-chain → fix failures → verify fix. Tight loop, no held remainder.
decisions.rationale: User wants immediate verification and correction, not a deferred retry queue. Each transfer is verified on Solana, failures are retried with adjusted parameters, and the fix is verified before moving on.

decisions.choice: Configurable per fund — skip divestment if basket TVL is below threshold
decisions.rationale: Gas + bridge fees on small amounts can eat the proceeds. Default minimum prevents uneconomical divestments.

decisions.choice: Match Bags.fm website and app store branding — seamless, very clean
decisions.rationale: User wants the app to feel native to Bags.fm, not a separate product. Branded consistently with the platform it lives in.

decisions.choice: Immutable divestment config, on-chain verifiability, holder snapshot at trigger time, Alvara-managed basket operations
decisions.rationale: User explicitly said "can't be rugged or gamed." Immutable config prevents owner from changing split. On-chain transparency lets holders verify. Trigger-time snapshot prevents buy-in gaming. Alvara handles basket custody.
